failure_modes.text: Teams can keep producing cleaner versions of the same answer when familiar frames remain unchallenged.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when familiar frames are producing stale options.
failure_modes.mitigation: Explicitly challenge the current frame before refining within it; cleaner versions of the same answer are not lateral thinking.

failure_modes.text: More analysis inside the current frame can waste time when the real blockage is conceptual rather than effort-based.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works when the blockage is conceptual, not effort-based.
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish effort from insight: more analysis inside the current frame is wasted when the real blockage is conceptual, not analytical.

failure_modes.text: Avoiding fixation can become overcorrection if the team rejects all prior knowledge instead of just interrupting stale lock-in.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the team treats avoid fixation as a reason to reject all prior knowledge.
failure_modes.mitigation: Interrupt stale lock-in without rejecting all prior knowledge; overcorrection that discards useful patterns is as wasteful as fixation.

premortem_questions.text: What assumption is keeping us inside the current frame even though it is no longer producing good options?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when familiar frames are producing stale options.

premortem_questions.text: If we reframed the implicit problem as a new question, what option space would reopen?
Are we widening possibilities before narrowing, or are we converging too early out of habit?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when divergence comes before convergence.

heuristics.text: Interrupt the dominant template early enough that alternative frames actually get airtime.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when the blockage is conceptual, not effort-based.

heuristics.text: Use reframed questions to prevent the first solution from anchoring the whole session.
heuristics.source_quote: If a solution arises prematurely, the playbook dictates reframing the implicit problem as a new HMW question.

heuristics.text: Delay convergence until the team has genuinely explored unconventional options.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when divergence comes before convergence.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the chunks close to hidden-assumption interruption and divergence-before-convergence.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor innovation-theater sprawl because the raw source already supports clean reframing chunks.
open_questions: The execution limitation is source-real but better handled as a note than as a first-order intervention chunk.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on reframing and outside-view style discipline.
antagonists: The source is strongest against representativeness-heuristic, status-quo-bias, and confirmation-bias.
structured_tensions: The model contains explicit tensions against representativeness-heuristic, status-quo-bias, and confirmation-bias.
